title: Scope epic: Standardize Taskulus configuration across Python and Rust
description: Define scope, behaviors, and configuration model for cross-implementation standardization (Python+Rust). Produce follow-up beads for implementation.
creator: Ryan Alyn Porter

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Scope change: dropped dotyaml crate epic (tskl-9nu closed). New approach: single .taskulus.yml at repo root, no fallbacks. Added task tskl-s3w to define behaviors/specs for the new file, including project directory key (default project/).

custom.beads_notes:
Goal: define shared configuration model for Taskulus (Python + Rust), leveraging Python dot-yml semantics; produce spec, behaviors, and follow-up beads for implementation. Outputs: agreed config keys (project key, workflows per issue type, priorities mapping/import), behaviors/Gherkin drafts, plan for Rust dot-yml equivalent and integration.
Created worktree ../Taskulus-config-standardization on branch tskl-oju-config-standardization for this session (BEADS_NO_DAEMON recommended). Next: study Python dotyaml behavior, design unified config keys (project key, workflows, priorities), draft Gherkin, and open follow-on beads.
Dependency noted: tskl-9nu (Rust dot-yml crate + publish to crates.io) must complete/land before configuration standardization finalization. Also related: tskl-pjb (publish Taskulus Rust crate).
Created child tasks: tskl-oju.1 (schema design), tskl-oju.2 (Gherkin behaviors).
Created follow-up tasks: tskl-oju.3 (Python loader), tskl-oju.4 (Rust loader), tskl-oju.5 (CLI override/dotenv parity).
Docs sweep in progress: renamed config references to taskulus.yml in README, CONFIGURATION, GETTING_STARTED, TROUBLESHOOTING, planning docs, web docs page, tools script, and rust doc comments; further code changes pending.
